name: Gregory Hines
abstract: Gregory Oliver Hines (February 14, 1946 – August 9, 2003) was an American dancer, actor, choreographer, and singer. He is one of the most celebrated tap dancers of all time.

concerts.label: From Harlem to Hollywood: A Tribute to the Nicholas Brothers "Tap Legends"
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Sherrie Maricle
about_quotes.evidence: I played with him, he managed Maurice and Gregory Hines. And there was a concert with Maurice and I was just hired to play drums. And he liked the way I played... a couple of years later he called me up
about_quotes.doc_title: Sherrie Maricle interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 18, 2001 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: We have a wonderful show that he did in the early ’80s with Gregory Hines singing and dancing, Freddie Hubbard, all of that stuff.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Luther Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: Pam Koslow who of course is Mrs. Gregory Hines... we had Gregory Hines and a cast that was fantastic
about_quotes.doc_title: Luther Henderson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dorothy Donegan
about_quotes.evidence: Gregory Hines, snatched the flowers out of my hand and ran off the stage.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dorothy Donegan — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
